title: Punta Cana 5-Day Beach Getaway
metaTitle: Punta Cana 5-Day Beach Getaway | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 5-day trip to Punta Cana, Dominican Republic: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Isla Saona day trip and Hoyo Azul cenote (Scape Park). Prices from $915 per person (estimate).
highlights: All-inclusive villa suite at a five-star Puntacana Resort
Private candlelit dinner on the beach
overview: Spend 5 days discovering Punta Cana, Dominican Republic — Tropical climate with a wetter, hurricane-prone season (Jun-Nov) and a warm, drier season (Dec-Apr) with consistent trade winds. December to April for dry, sunny weather and lower humidity, avoiding peak hurricane season. This itinerary blends Isla Saona day trip, Hoyo Azul cenote (Scape Park), Bavaro Beach with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
4 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Isla Saona day trip, Hoyo Azul cenote (Scape Park), Bavaro Beach
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Punta Cana
itinerary.description: Land at Punta Cana International Airport (PUJ) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Isla Saona day trip — Catamaran tour to a palm-lined island beach. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Punta Cana: Hoyo Azul cenote (Scape Park) & Altos de Chavón artist village
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Hoyo Azul cenote (Scape Park) — Turquoise natural sinkhole pool. In the afternoon, continue to Altos de Chavón artist village — Recreated 16th-century Mediterranean village and amphitheater. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Punta Cana: Marinarium snorkeling boat tour & Anfiteatro del Mar zipline (Scape Park)
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Marinarium snorkeling boat tour — Boat trip with nurse shark and stingray encounters. In the afternoon, continue to Anfiteatro del Mar zipline (Scape Park) — Zipline over caves and jungle. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Isla Saona
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Isla Saona, a popular side trip from Punta Cana. Return to Punta Cana in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Manatí Park or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to PUJ for your departure flight.

travelTips: Bring small USD bills, as tipping is expected even at all-inclusives
Book Isla Saona and Hoyo Azul tours through reputable operators in advance
Agree a taxi fare before departure since meters aren't used
Resort day passes let you sample multiple properties without booking a full stay
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
A light rain layer for sudden tropical showers
Small USD bills for tipping resort staff and vendors
thingsToAvoid: Buying excursions from unlicensed street vendors instead of your resort or a reputable operator
Swimming during red-flag warnings, especially during hurricane season (Jun-Nov)
Overpaying unmetered taxis without agreeing a fare
Wandering far from tourist areas alone at night
familyActivities: Manatí Park dolphin and marine show
luxuryExperiences: All-inclusive villa suite at a five-star Puntacana Resort
Private yacht day trip to Isla Catalina
Golf at the Corales Golf Course
honeymoonExperiences: Private candlelit dinner on the beach
Couples spa treatment at a resort
Secluded swim in the Hoyo Azul cenote
budgetExperiences: Free public beach access at Playa El Cortecito
Self-guided walk along the Los Corales boardwalk
Resort day pass deals to sample a property
localEtiquette: Tipping is expected for resort staff even at all-inclusives — bring small USD bills.
Greet warmly with a handshake or cheek kiss.
Punctuality runs relaxed ("Dominican time") — build in buffer.
Bargaining is expected at craft markets, less so in fixed-price shops.
localTransportGuide: Most visitors stay within all-inclusive resort complexes and book excursions through hotel tour desks; taxis are the standard way to leave the resort, with fares agreed in advance since they're unmetered. Público (shared van) transport serves locals but isn't typically geared toward tourists.
bestTimeToVisit: December to April for dry, sunny weather and lower humidity, avoiding peak hurricane season.
weather: Tropical climate with a wetter, hurricane-prone season (Jun-Nov) and a warm, drier season (Dec-Apr) with consistent trade winds.
visaInformation: Visa-free entry for most tourist nationalities (US, EU, UK, Canada) for stays up to 30 days; the former tourist card fee is now generally included in airfare or immigration processing, but a valid passport is required.
averageFlightTime: ~15.6h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Punta Cana?
faqs.answer: Visa-free entry for most tourist nationalities (US, EU, UK, Canada) for stays up to 30 days; the former tourist card fee is now generally included in airfare or immigration processing, but a valid passport is required.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Punta Cana?
faqs.answer: December to April for dry, sunny weather and lower humidity, avoiding peak hurricane season. Tropical climate with a wetter, hurricane-prone season (Jun-Nov) and a warm, drier season (Dec-Apr) with consistent trade winds.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Punta Cana safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Punta Cana is safe for tourists who stay alert, particularly around tourist hotspots and transport hubs — avoid displaying valuables and use hotel-recommended transport after dark.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Punta Cana?
faqs.answer: The local currency is DOP. Carry some cash for smaller vendors — card acceptance can be inconsistent outside major hotels and malls.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: Yes — Punta Cana is one of our most-booked honeymoon destinations, especially the Private candlelit dinner on the beach experience. It also works well for families — highlights include Manatí Park dolphin and marine show.

reviews.author: Priya Smith
reviews.title: Great for families with kids
reviews.body: Booked this as a solo trip and it was well paced. Veron local market in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Anna Williams
reviews.title: Perfect for our honeymoon
reviews.body: Punta Cana is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Deep-sea fishing charter. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Anna Smith
reviews.title: Smooth from start to finish
reviews.body: Punta Cana is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Playa Macao. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: David Garcia
reviews.title: Great value for money
reviews.body: Punta Cana is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Cabeza de Toro fishing village. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: James Larsen
reviews.title: Would book again in a heartbeat
reviews.body: Our trip to Punta Cana was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Isla Saona day trip was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Ava Botha
reviews.title: Loved every moment
reviews.body: Isla Saona day trip alone was worth the trip, but combined with Veron local market this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.
